name: Claude Code session and compaction behavior
context_primitives: Long-running terminal/IDE agent combining repository access, bash, file tools, and multi-turn history. When context pressure grows, sessions rely on compaction-style behavior (summarizing prior work) described in third-party analyses and aligned with Anthropic's API compaction feature for programmatic use.
deployment_pattern: Local CLI or editor integration; project instructions via CLAUDE.md and committed config; users can invoke manual compaction where supported. Actual token accounting is opaque to end users beyond visible thread limits.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: Evaluated indirectly via coding benchmarks (SWE-bench style) in industry leaderboards; no single public 'compaction quality' metric.
ecosystem_integrations: Reads git workspaces, MCP servers, and CI logs; pairs with Anthropic API features when using cloud models.
differentiators: Tight coupling of repo tools + conversation; productized workflow vs. rolling your own ReAct loop.
significance_for_builders: Assume lossy long sessions: checkpoint intent in CLAUDE.md, write handoff notes at task boundaries, and avoid relying on megabytes of prior tool output after compaction.
key_timeline_or_milestones: Anthropic API compaction beta (2026 header in docs) clarifies server-side pattern many agent products mirror.

primary_sources.title: Compaction — Claude API (reference for compaction model)
